design_summary: Entity extraction: prompted local Ollama completion (llama3.1:8b) via the existing LLMClient interface, JSON-array response parsed with json_fences.strip_code_fences/sanitize_control_chars_and_triple_quotes (reused, not reimplemented); non-primary capitalized-run heuristic fallback only on unparseable output. Graph representation: stdlib-only dataclass EntityGraph with three dict[str, set[str]] adjacency maps (entity->docs, entity->co-occurring entities, doc->entities) -- no new dependency (no networkx). retrieve_documents matches vector_rag.retrieve_documents's list[str] ranked-doc-id output shape; resolves query entities against the graph (exact + substring-containment fallback for paraphrase tolerance), then does a capped-hop BFS (DEFAULT_MAX_HOPS=1) with per-hop score decay (HOP_DECAY=0.5) as the disclosed mitigation for docs/HLD.md #7's graph-traversal-blow-up risk.
test_results: 21/21 new tests pass (9 pure-unit, 6 deterministic stub-LLMClient, 6 live-local against real Ollama), reproduced 3x with no flakiness observed. 83/83 across the full agents/eval/ suite (62 pre-existing + 21 new), no regressions. ruff check clean.
prompt_iteration_disclosed: The initial entity-extraction prompt returned an empty JSON array for short question-style queries lacking formal proper nouns (e.g. 'How do I get a new password if I'm locked out of my account?'), which silently broke retrieval for those queries. Fixed during self-consistency by broadening the prompt to explicitly request noun-phrase concepts/topics in addition to proper nouns, with a worked question-style example. Also fixed the heuristic fallback's regex picking up sentence-initial 'The' as part of an entity string.
not_verified_by_self: Per invariant I4, this implementer did not verify its own work. Handoff is for /cdr:verify to independently re-run agents/eval/test_graphrag_baseline.py and confirm the disclosed reasoning.
open_items_for_verifier: Confirm the entity-extraction prompt's reliability across repeated live runs on the verifier's own Ollama instance -- LLM-prompted extraction is inherently less deterministic than 5.2.1's embeddings even at temperature=0.0; this run observed 3/3 reproducible passes but that is not an exhaustive guarantee.
Confirm no existing file (vector_rag.py, vector_rag_rerank.py, agents/llm/*, agents/pyproject.toml) was modified -- git diff shows additions only (two new files).

Confirm the HOP_DECAY=0.5 / DEFAULT_MAX_HOPS=1 blow-up-mitigation design in architecture-discovery.md section 3 is sound and sufficiently disclosed, and that the substring-containment entity-matching fallback is a genuine (non-strawman, non-overly-permissive) design choice rather than one that trivially inflates recall.

Confirm the fixture corpus/query set in test_graphrag_baseline.py's live tier is a fair, discriminating test of entity-graph retrieval (not pre-rigged toward success), consistent with 5.2.1's/5.2.2's own fairness-disclosure standard.
